units.title: Feelings & Emotions
units.subtitle: Happy, Sad, Angry, Sleepy, Hungry, Thirsty
grammar_focus: Yes, I am / No, I am not.

lessons.title: How Are You?
lessons.topic: Basic emotions and How are you?

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What does the boy do when he is feeling sad?
questions.hint: It's something you do when you're not feeling happy.

questions.question_text: What sound does the boy hear when he's outside?
questions.hint: Listen for a happy morning sound!

questions.question_text: What makes the boy smile in the video?
questions.hint: It's an activity you enjoy with others!

items.example: How are you? I am happy.
Are you sad? No, I am not.

steps.title: Amy and Tom's Day
steps.text: Hello, Amy! How do you feel? I am happy. How are you, Tom? I am sad. Where is my teddy bear?

steps.explanation: Use this to talk about how you feel.
examples: How are you? I am excited.
How are you? I am sleepy.
How are you? I am okay.

items.sentence: How am you? I am happy.
How are you? I is sad.

steps.audio_text: Hi! How are you feeling today? I am sad because it is raining outside.

questions.question_text: I ___ happy. (Grammar question)
What does 'sad' mean? (Vocabulary question)
True or False: 'How are you?' asks about feelings.
What might you say if you are feeling good? (Reading comprehension question)

lessons.title: I Want Water
lessons.topic: Physical needs and Are you...? questions

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What do you feel when you need water?
questions.hint: It's when your mouth is dry.

questions.question_text: What is something you drink every day?
questions.hint: It's clear and comes from the tap.

questions.question_text: Where do you play with a slide and swing?
questions.hint: You can play outside in this place.

items.example: Are you hungry? Yes, I am.
How are you? I am thirsty.
Do you want to eat? Yes, I do.
Are you thirsty? Yes, I will drink.

steps.title: Park Adventure
steps.text: How are you? I am hungry. Are you thirsty? Yes, I am. Let's eat a sandwich and drink some juice. Are you happy now? Yes, I am!

steps.explanation: Use this to talk about feelings.
examples: Are you sleepy? Yes, I am.
Are you happy? Yes, I am.
Are you cold? No, I am not.

items.sentence: Are you thirsty? No, I ___ not.
Are you are happy? Yes, I am.

steps.audio_text: Are you hungry, Tom? No, I am thirsty. Can I drink some water, please?

questions.question: What does Tom want to drink?

questions.question_text: Choose the right word: I ___ hungry. (Grammar question)
What word means you need something to drink? (Vocabulary question)
Fill in the blank: Are you ___? Yes, I am thirsty.
True or False: You say 'No, I am not' if you are thirsty. (Reading comprehension)
True or False: You ask 'Are you hungry?' to check if someone wants to eat. (Reading comprehension)

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: How is the cat feeling after eating?
questions.hint: Think about how you feel after eating food.

questions.question_text: What do you do when you are tired?
What sound does a happy cat make?
questions.hint: It sounds like a soft engine.

items.example: Are you angry? Yes, I am.
How are you? I am sleepy.
Are you mad? No, I am not.
It is night. I am sleepy.

steps.title: Sleepy Night
steps.text: It is night. I am sleepy. My dog is sleepy too. Are you mad? No, I am not. I am happy.

steps.explanation: Ask and answer how you feel.
examples: Are you sleepy? Yes, I am.
Are you angry? No, I am not.
Are you happy? Yes, I am.

items.sentence: My face is ___. I am angry.
My face is ___. I am happy.
My face is red. I am feel angry.
My face are yellow. I am happy.

steps.audio_text: Are you angry, Tom? No, I am not angry. I am just sleepy. It's time for bed now.

questions.question: Is it time for breakfast or bedtime?

steps.prompt: Say: My face is red. I am mad.
steps.expected_text: my face is red. i am mad.

questions.question_text: What is another word for 'mad'? (Vocabulary question)
My face is ___. I am sleepy. (Grammar question)
What time is it if you feel sleepy? (Reading comprehension question)
True or False: If someone is 'sleepy', they want to play.
What comes next: 'I am [feeling]. My face is [___].'

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What does your body need when you feel thirsty?
questions.hint: It's something you drink when you're thirsty.

questions.question_text: What feeling do you get after playing in the sun?
questions.hint: You need to drink after this feeling.

questions.question_text: Which of these is the best choice for feeling refreshed?
questions.hint: It's something wet that you can drink.

steps.title: A Happy Day
steps.text: Hi, I am Lucy. How are you? I am happy because I am playing. Are you happy too? Yes, I am!

steps.audio_text: Hi there! How do you feel today? I feel excited because I'm going to the park. Do you like going to the park?

steps.prompt: Say: How are you feeling now and why?
steps.expected_text: i am happy because i am playing

questions.question_text: How are you? I ___ happy.
Which emoji shows 'happy'? (Vocabulary question)
True or False: 'No, I am not.' answers 'Are you sad?'
What do you say if you are not feeling well?
Fill in the blank: Are you ____(feeling)?
